text:
Once upon a time in a bustling city park, there was a fox named Felix. Felix wasn't just any fox; he loved reading Shakespeare's sonnets late into the moonlit night.

One evening, a big, black owl named Olga swooped down on Felix. Olga had a terrible head cold and felt like murdering everyone she saw. Felix, however, merely rolled his eyes and continued his nocturnal reading.

Felix wasn't interested in typical owl gossip. He loved stories about heroes. One tale that enchanted him was of a fox who became a hero.

Felix imagined himself dressing like this heroic fox—he'd wear a red cloak like the story said—and saving the enchanted forest full of talking animals with a big stick.

He snuck around during daylight pretending to be nice. Whenever he saw a mouse peeking out anxiously he'd leap out, howl, and give a big hug, forgetting to warn them about the big stick dangling from his hand.
